summary: Strong West L.A., Shakedown; uneven vocals & Brent's fade
blurb: The first set bursts with punchy West L.A. Fadeaway, Me And My Uncle and Mexicali Blues, while Shakedown Street shines in the second set. Vocal delivery is inconsistent, especially on Tons of Steel and That's All Right, Mama, and Brent's voice wanes later. The soundboard capture is clear, highlighting crisp drums and a tight rhythm section.
review: April 18 1986 at Berkeley Community Theater finds the Dead delivering a mixed bag of high‑energy moments and vocal hiccups. Jerry’s guitar work remains sharp on numbers like West L.A. Fadeaway and Shakedown Street, and his picking on “That’s All Right, Mama” is commendable even if the vocal delivery falters. Phil lays down solid bass—though a bit low in the mix typical of ’80s boards—and belts out strong vocals on Mexicali Blues. Brent supplies tasteful organ textures on Smokestack Lightning but his voice drifts off around three‑quarters through “Tons Of Steel” and later songs, leaving a noticeable gap. Bob adds color with wah‑pedal accents, while Mickey and Billy keep the percussion crisp and driving throughout. The show’s highlights—West L.A., Mexicali Blues, Shakedown Street, Alabama > Greatest Story, He’s Gone, and Smokestack Lightning—are captured well on the recommended Miller soundboard tape, though listeners should be aware of uneven vocal performances that temper an otherwise solid set.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Analyses show mixed sentiment with both praise and criticism; no consistent strong positive or negative trend, so neutral adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with crisp drums, clear mix and minimal technical issues; showcases the strongest performances of the night.

key_highlights: Punchy West L.A. Fadeaway kicks off the set with high energy.
Shakedown Street delivers a funky vibe in the second set.
Alabama > Greatest Story sequence flows smoothly and highlights tight transitions.
Crisp drum work and solid rhythm section are captured especially well on the recommended soundboard.

band_performance.Jerry: Solid picking and inventive wah‑pedal textures; vocals sound tired on several numbers and are low in the audience mix, but guitar presence remains strong.
band_performance.Phil: Bass a bit quiet in the board mix but delivers powerful lines, especially on Mexicali Blues; vocal contribution is loud and confident.
band_performance.Bob: Adds interesting wah‑pedal chord colors; otherwise no major issues noted.
band_performance.Brent: Provides notable organ work on Smokestack Lightning and adds texture throughout; voice fades around “Tons Of Steel” and later, leading to weaker vocal moments.
band_performance.Mickey: Hi‑hats and snares captured crisp and clean, contributing to the overall tight percussion feel.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming is clear and powerful, complementing Mickey’s percussive drive.
